name: Answer indexed documents with HTTP / REST API for Q&A

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf06663-document`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: List Changed Docs (API)

### List Changed Docs (API)

GET https://api.example.com/v1/documents — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Remove Duplicate Documents

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Remove Duplicate Documents

Drops duplicates within this run (compared on `docId`).

Next: Attach Chunk Metadata

### Attach Chunk Metadata

Custom JavaScript: Attach chunk-level metadata before embedding

Next: Insert into Vector Store

### Insert into Vector Store

Writes embedded chunks into the vector index.

Next: Count Indexed Chunks

🔑 Qdrant URL + key

### Prepare Documents

Wraps incoming items as documents (with metadata) for the vector store.

Feeds **Insert into Vector Store** as `document`

### Chunk with Overlap

Chunks documents (~1200 chars, 120 overlap) so retrieval hits stay focused.

Feeds **Prepare Documents** as `textSplitter`

### Document Embeddings

Embedding model — MUST be the same model for indexing and querying, or retrieval quality collapses.

Feeds **Insert into Vector Store** as `embedding`

🔑 OpenAI API key

### Count Indexed Chunks

Collapses all items into a single summary item (for counts and run stats).

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#knowledge`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Answer indexed documents with HTTP / REST API for Q&A

**Pattern:** RAG Pipeline · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Answer indexed documents with HTTP / REST API for Q&A. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual q&a is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → List Changed Docs (API) → Remove Duplicate Documents → Attach Chunk Metadata → Insert into Vector Store → Count Indexed Chunks → Send Summary to Slack

## Use it for
- Teams that want to rag pipeline without manual effort
- Anyone automating q&a in ai
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Standardizes the process every time
- Removes copy-paste errors

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; OpenAI API key; Qdrant URL + key; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Ingestion is a separate workflow from querying because indexing and answering have different triggers, schedules, and failure modes — a slow re-index should never block a live user question. Chunking with overlap (not whole documents) keeps each retrieval hit focused on relevant text instead of dragging in an entire document's unrelated sections.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Change chunkSize and chunkOverlap, then compare retrieval quality back in the query workflow.
2. Add a second file source and normalize both into the same chunk-metadata shape.
3. Swap the embedding model — but change it in both this workflow and the query workflow, since they must match.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your q&a process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
